observations.recommendation:
1.17. We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation for the Municipal
Mayor to compel the Inventory Committee, through a written directive with
timelines and sanctions, to:

a) Accomplish and complete in CY 2024 the comprehensive physical
count of all properties owned by the Municipality, from the preparation of the
Physical Inventory Plan until the submission of the Report of Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report on the Physical Count of the Local
Road Network (RPCLRN) in the required formats to the Municipal
Accountant before the year-end closing of the LGU books of accounts;

b) Conduct an investigation on non-existing/missing PPEs and prepare a
separate report thereon for immediate submission to the Municipal
Accountant as a pre-requisite for the filing of the request for derecognition.

We further recommended that:

a. The Municipal Accountant derecognize from the PPE accounts those
items costing less than ₱50,000.00, and reclassify them to the appropriate
accounts supported with Inventory Custodian Slips; and

b. The Supply Officer issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slips to
the end-users of serviceable items costing less than the capitalization
threshold, which have been derecognized from PPE.

We also reiterated the following recommendations:

a) That the Municipal Accountant maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards for
each category of PPE and reconcile the same with the Property Cards of the
Property Unit, and the RPCPPE and RPCLRN; and

b) That the Property Officer maintain updated Property Cards and reconcile
the same with the PPELCs of the Accounting Office with the RPCPPE and
RPCLRN.

2.12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry
in the Trust Fund books to record the additional amount of ₱6,562,503.15 in
both the Inter-Agency Receivable account Due from Other Funds, and the
Trust Liabilities account Trust Liabilities – DRRM.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to process immediately the remittance of the
unutilized PDAF and CDF Funds of ₱694,718.18 to the Bureau of Treasury.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately to the Bureau of Treasury the
shares of the National Meat Inspection Services from meat inspection fees
collected by the Municipality totaling ₱2,671,237.53.

5.11 We recommend that, henceforth, the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the
Municipal Peace and Order Council, mobilize the Council and its Technical
Working Group to:

a) formulate the Peace and Order and Public Safety (POPS) Plan and endorse it
to the Sannguniang Bayan (SB) in time for the latter to adopt it and ensure
that the funds are timely provided in the budget;
b) if the Council deems that the peace and order situation calls for the use of CF,
secure, PRIOR to allocating CF, the required certification of the Burauen
Chief of Police containing the concrete detail of the circumstances that
warrant the allocation of Confidential Fund (CF); and
c) require the preparation of the Physical and Financial Plan to support the
proposed allocation for CF to be submitted to the SB for funding.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer
review the proposed annual budget to make sure that:

d) The allocation for Peace and Order Programs (POP) does not exceed the
total estimated amount of the Peace and Order policies, programs,
projects, services, and activities in the POPS Plan; and
e) The allocation for CF does not exceed the allowable amount equivalent
to 30% of the POP allocation.

6.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all personnel concerned
continuously adhere to the provisions of the cited JMC to ensure the maximum
attainment of the desired socio-economic development and environmental
management outcome of the Municipality.

7.5 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of using her
collections to encash the checks issued by the LGU to its officials and
personnel, and deposit all collections intact.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
(i) Submit to the Audit Team the General Journals, together with the original copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) and its supporting documents, for CY 2022 and every year thereafter, on the
(ii) Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for Accounts Payable under Special Education Fund to provide the details of each account; and
(iii) Review and analyze the negative amounts and the discrepancies between the financial statements, the General and Subsidiary Ledgers and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile both records and resolve the abnormal balances.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
2. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
(i) Adjust the Medical Equipment account and additions in the months of September and October 2022, respectively and correct the erroneous addition of ₱285,300.00 and the related accounts with supporting documents to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary records for the cited PPE sub-accounts.
(ii) Prepare and maintain the subsidiary ledgers for the cited sub-accounts.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal documents to support the additions made in the Medical Equipment account amounting to ₱196,000.00 ₱285,300.00 and ₱89,300.00 in the months of September and October 2022, respectively were not found.

observations.recommendation:
3. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
(i) Reconcile their respective cashbooks and accounting records monthly for all funds; and
(ii) Reconcile at least quarterly the cashbooks and accounting records, especially at the year-end, to report, determine errors and omissions in recording, and avoid discrepancies noted as bases to effect the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction: Implemented
observations.reason: But for the other errors committed has a cumulative effect over long periods. The Accounting Office already made some adjustments and is still gathering data to verify and effect the necessary adjustments in the books.

observations.recommendation:
4. We recommended that the Local Development Council approve a project for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan after the proponent office, in coordination with other relevant offices/ officials, has subjected the proposed project to comprehensive planning, which includes consultation with stakeholders and clearing legal impediments in the projects’ implementation.
5. We further recommended that the Local Finance Committee review the balances of the Continuing Appropriations during the planning and preparation of the Annual Budget in order that unexpended balances, including savings from completed project, which are eligible for reversion and re-programming can be duly processed, and the funds will be put to proper use instead of being kept idle.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
6. We recommended that:
The Municipal DRRM Office prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the said report to the Accounting Office on or before the 10th day of the following month, and submit a copy of the report, duly certified by the Municipal Accountant, to the COA Audit Team;
7. The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Office and Municipal DRRM Office reconcile their records on DRRMF monthly or quarterly, to determine the remaining balances; and
8. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, transfer cash in the equivalent amount of the unexpended balance of the Current Appropriation – QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the bank account for the Special Trust Fund before the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
9. The heads of departments and operating units, at the helm, in coordination with the HRMO, assess the agency-wide personnel needs and create a staffing pattern, and ensure the number of COS and JOs hired was in line with said staffing plan.
10. The Personnel Selection Board and the HRMO should base the hiring of COS and JOs on the approved staffing pattern, and where provided indicate the specific job description in the contracts of the Job Orders to delineate responsibilities, and avoid duplication and/or gaps in the scope of work.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constituted an Inventory and Disposal Committee to: ➢ prepare, for the Mayor’s approval, a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the assignments of Committee members, schedules/location of taking and cut-off dates for the complete physical inventory over a three-month period in CY 2022; ➢ implement the PIP and finish the physical inventory within the time frame specified in CY 2022; and ➢ submit RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant before the end of CY 2022 following the Procedural Guidelines provided in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, for reconciliation with the book balances; 13. the Municipal Accountant maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE and reconcile the same with the Property Cards annually; and 14. the Property Officer maintain complete Property Cards and reconcile the same with the PPELC of the accounting unit based on the RPCPPE and RCLRN that will be submitted by the Inventory Committee.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: However, the Committee still has not undertaken the physical count of the PPE. This audit observation has been reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: 15. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile immediately their respective Cash in Bank balances against the balances per bank statements, or the Municipal Treasurer's cashbook and the GL/SL balances of Cash - Local Treasury for all funds; 16. the Municipal Treasurer prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation within 10 days from receipt of the bank statements and submit the same monthly to the Audit Team together with the corresponding bank statements for review; and 17. the Municipal Treasurer prepare a monthly reconciliation of each bank account balance with the related subsidiary ledger balance.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
18. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
(i) reconcile the CIP balances at year-end with the records of the Municipal Engineering Office for all on-going construction projects to ensure the accuracy of the CIP balances that will be reflected in the Statement of Financial Position; and
(ii) prepare the necessary journal entry to correct the Accumulated Depreciation accounts for any overstatement of depreciation on the School Buildings and Water Supply Systems.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request from the donor agencies the actual quantities and the unit cost of the vaccines that will receive in the future, and record the same and provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from the LPHO relative to the receipt of the vaccines, including the data on the doses administered at the end of each month. 20. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the perpetual inventory system, the vaccines that will be received by the LGU and the doses that will be dispensed, using as reference the report and documents submitted by the MHO.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Officer: (i) Identify programs, projects and activities on disaster management to be funded from the unutilized balances and incorporate said PPAs in the LDRRMFIP; and (ii) Implement all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP in order to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and strengthen the capability of the LGU and its constituents in disaster management. 22. We also recommended that the MDRRM Officer: (i) keep a record and monitor the utilization and balances of LDRRM Fund; (ii) include all the components of the Fund in the preparation of the annual LDRRM Fund Investment Plan, until it is approved by the Local Sanggunian; (iii) monitor the implementation of the programmed PPAs, review monthly the feasibility of the PPAs for implementation and propose revision when warranted; and (iv) prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and submit a copy to the Commission of Audit, through the assigned Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all Heads of Offices instructing that receipts and deliveries of all procured supplies and materials should be centralized and handled by the Supply Officer for monitoring and proper recording; 24. We also recommended that the Supply Officer: (i) Prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) duly supported with pre-numbered RIS issued during each month and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant every 1st week of the month to reduce his accountability; and (ii) Maintain stock cards and record all supplies upon receipt of deliveries supported with the Acceptance and Inspection Report. 25. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards to record receipts, issuances and balances of supplies procured by the LGU. 26. Lastly, we recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of all inventory items every end of the year and submit a complete RPCI to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with the balances in the Supplies Ledger Card.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to: (i) Require the official who received the cash donations to prepare a donations report on how the funds were utilized and to turn-over to the Municipal Treasurer any unused amounts for the issuance of Official Receipt; (ii) Require the officials who received donations in-kind to prepare a report on the items received in CY 2020, to whom it was issued and whether there are balances still on hand; and (iii) Designate a specific official to take charge of donations, either in cash and in-kind, who will maintain the necessary records and prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ascertain the actual weather condition existing in the project site through the Project Engineer In-charge where projects are implemented and consider other documentary evidence prior to approval of the contractor’s request for time extension of work; 29. The Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the grant of time extension of work in accordance with Items 10.2 and 10.5 of Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184. 30. Management should observe the procedure in determining the contract time or duration during the conduct of detailed engineering and disclose in the Instruction to Bidders the estimated number of rainy/unworkable days considered unfavorable for the prosecution of the works at site before the date of bidding pursuant to Item 3(j) of Annex A of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit their comments and viable reasons as to the questioned participation to the competitive bidding as further outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184. Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of perfected contracts to the Audit Team within five days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The BAC submitted their comments and viable reasons which is still subject for further validation by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: 32. We recommended that management adopt the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) facility of AGDBs to safeguard wages, and other government funds and property.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 33. We recommended that the management request for authority to write-off dormant accounts from the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The management has yet to file requesting for the write-off of the dormant accounts from the books.

observations.recommendation: 34. We recommended that the concerned officers and employees be required to submit copies of approved Purchase Orders to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance, and the official in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items be required to furnish the auditor copies of delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 36. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (i) Ensure the proper recording of the transfer, receipt of fund which includes the costs incurred to construct and complete the PPE, and liquidation thereof for it to be completely accounted for in the books of accounts; and (ii) Cause the recording of the PPE account in the books and accounts of the LGU-Barangay upon turn-over and acceptance supported by the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The office of the Municipal Engineer has yet to retrieve the certificate of completion and acceptance of the completed projects, particularly the recipients of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) Submit to the Audit Team the General Journals, together with the original
copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) and its supporting documents,
for CY 2022 and every year thereafter, on the Accounts Payable recorded
at year-end totaling ₱44,518,657.41 to prove that there are valid claimants;
otherwise, revert the amounts to the unappropriated surplus;
b.) Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for Accounts Payable under
Special Education Fund to provide the details of each account; and
c.) Review and analyze the negative amounts and the discrepancies between
the financial statements, the General and Subsidiary Ledgers and make the
necessary adjustments to reconcile both records and resolve the abnormal
balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Adjust the Medical Equipment account to correct the erroneous addition of
₱285,300.00 and the related depreciation expenses that may have been
taken up during the year, and
b) Prepare and maintain the subsidiary ledgers for the cited sub-accounts
with no subsidiary records and provide copies to the Auditor to facilitate
the verification of the balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Review immediately the noted discrepancies and prepare promptly the
appropriate adjustments to correct the cashbooks and the GL/SL balances
of Cash – Local Treasury for all funds; and
b) Reconcile at least quarterly the cashbooks and accounting records, most
especially at year-end, to immediately determine errors and omissions in
recording, and avoid complicated variances that are difficult and time-
consuming to resolve since errors committed has a cumulative effect over
long periods.

We recommended that the Local Development Council approves a project for
inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan after the proponent office, in
coordination with other relevant offices/officials, has subjected the proposed
project to comprehensive planning, which includes consultation with the
various stakeholders and clearing legal impediments in the projects’
implementation.

We further recommended that the Local Finance Committee review the
balances of the Continuing Appropriations during the planning and
preparation of the Annual Budget in order that unexpended balances,
including savings from completed project, which are eligible for reversion and
re-programming can be duly processed, and the funds will be put to proper use
instead of being kept idle.

(a) The Municipal DRRM Office prepare the monthly Report on the Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the said report to the Accounting
Office on or before the 10th day of the following month, and submit a copy
of the report, duly certified by the Municipal Accountant, to the COA Audit
Team;
(b) The Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal Budget Office and Municipal
DRRM Office reconcile their records on DRRMF monthly or quarterly, to
determine the remaining balances; and
(c) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,
transfer cash in the equivalent amount of the unexpended balance of the
Current Appropriation – QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the bank account for
the Special Trust Fund before the end of each year.

We recommended that:
(a) The heads of departments and operating units, with the Municipal Mayor
at the helm, and in coordination with the HRMO, assess the agency-wide
personnel needs and create a staffing pattern,
(b) The Personnel Selection Board and the HRMO should base the hiring of
COS and JOs on the approved staffing pattern, and
(c) The HRMO should indicate in the contracts of the Job Orders the specific
job descriptions to delineate responsibilities, and avoid duplication and/or
gaps in the scope of work.

1.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. create a new Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of
members that will be committed to conduct a complete annual physical
count of LGU properties;
b. issue a directive to the newly created Inventory Committee to:
1) prepare, for the Mayor’s approval, a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP)
containing the assignments of Committee members, schedules/location
of inventory-taking and cut-off dates for the complete physical
inventory of all properties of the Municipality over a three-month
period in CY 2022;
2) implement the PIP and finish the physical inventory within the time
frame specified in CY 2022; and
3) submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant before the end of CY
2022 following the Procedural Guidelines provided COA Circular
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015, for reconciliation with the book
balances.

1.10. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain PPE Ledger
Card for each category of PPE and reconcile the same with the Property Cards
annually; and

1.11. We recommended further that the Property Officer maintain complete
Property Cards and reconcile the same with the PPELC of the accounting unit
based on the RPCPPE and RCLRN that will be submitted by the Inventory
Committee.

2.12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer reconcile immediately their respective Cash in Bank balances
against the balances per bank records.

2.13 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement within 10 days from receipt of the
bank statements or bank snapshot and submit the same monthly to the Audit
Team for review together with the corresponding bank statements or bank
snapshot of the transactions for the month.

2.14. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare a monthly
reconciliation of each bank account balance with the related subsidiary ledger
balance.

3.9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant
d) record, henceforth, under the Construction in Progress (CIP) account,
all payments for progress billings of projects during the construction
period;
e) reconcile the CIP balances at year-end with the records of the
Municipal Engineering Office for all on-going construction projects to
ensure the accuracy of the CIP balances that will be reflected in the
Statement of Financial Position; and
f) prepare the necessary journal entry to correct the Accumulated
Depreciation accounts for any overstatement of depreciation on the
School Buildings and Water Supply Systems.

4.11. We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer:

c) Request from the donor agencies the actual information on the unit cost
and quantities of the COVID 19 vaccines that the LGU received, and will
receive in the future, and record the same in the appropriate a logbook to
account the receipt and distribution of said vaccines; and
d) Provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete documents from the
LPHO relative to the receipt of the vaccines, including the data on the doses
administered at the end of each month.

4.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record using the
perpetual inventory system, the vaccines that will be received by the LGU and
the doses that will be dispensed, using as reference the report and documents
submitted by the MHO.

5.18. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the Municipal DRRM
Council, through the MDRRM Officer:

c) identify programs, projects and activities on disaster management to be
funded from the unutilized ₱1,071,980.74 in the Special Trust Fund and
the unutilized ₱2,736,385.56 in the Continuing Appropriations for Capital
Outlay, and incorporate said PPAs in the LDRRMFIP; and
d) implement all PPAs in the LDRRMFIP in order to maximize the utilization
of the LDRRM Fund and strengthen the capability of the LGU and its
constituents in disaster management.

5.19. We also recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer:

f) request the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to provide him
with the balances of all components of the LDRRM Fund;
g) keep a record and monitor the utilization and balances of LDRRM Fund;
h) include all the components of the Fund in the preparation of the annual
LDRRM Fund Investment Plan, until it is approved by the Local
Sanggunian;
i) monitor the implementation of the programmed PPAs, review monthly the
feasibility of the PPAs for implementation and propose revisions when
warranted; and
j) prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund and submit a copy to the Commission
on Audit, through the assigned Audit Team, in compliance with Item 5.1.5
of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

6.8. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer inspect the actual
accomplishment of the contractor on the project to determine the percentage
of completion, compute the negative slippage incurred, verify if there is
sufficient basis to terminate the contract and initiate the calibrated processes
provided in GPPB Circular.

7.12. We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and Municipal
Accountant submit the following documents to validate the eligibility and
entitlement of the claimants and support the correctness of the amounts paid:

c) Duly accomplished DTRs, or equivalent documents, of all recipients of
COVID-19 AHDP and SRA, as evidence of the days that the HWs and
HRHs physically reported for work at their assigned work stations; and
d) Certification of the Municipal Health Officer that the claimants were
assigned at the Rural Health Unit or in the LGU’s quarantine facility.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, in the appropriation and obligation of extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses (discretionary fund) in order to prevent the incurrence of obligations without legal basis.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, LGU still allocated P1,690,000.00 for EME which exceeds the prescribed limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Department Heads concerned be required to submit an Accomplishment/Post-Activity Report to the COA Audit Team to be consolidated by the GAD Focal Person in order to ascertain the propriety of the expenditures totaling P 1,986,872.44 in accordance with Sections 4(6) and 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Reports is yet to be submitted to the auditor. Management is aware of the importance of the needed reports for submission to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Failure of the departments concerned to submit the related reports in the conduct of CIVAC on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council be directed to include in its LDRRMF Investment Plan only programs/ projects/ activities that are related to disaster management activities in order to ensure that the LDRRMF is appropriated for valid programs/projects/ activities only, pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121, Items A & B of DILG MC No. 2012-73 and Item 5 of the NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-01. Likewise, stop the practice of charging unrelated expenses to the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II, audit finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.managementAction: The Engineering Office already had initial listings for verification by the Accounting Unit.
observations.reason: No reason(s) was given by management for its non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management file a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts totaling P916,731.73 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016
observations.managementAction: Records needed to request for write-off unavailable.
observations.reason: Management is yet to submit request to write-off dormant accounts. Absence of documents to support such request  that would warrant writing-off dormant accounts  hindered management to take actions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to submit copies of approved Purchase Orders to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Likewise, require the official in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items to furnish the auditor copies of delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted copy of bidded contracts but not the Purchase Order and  Inspection and Acceptance Report.
observations.reason:
Perfected contracts were not submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution.

Management is yet to come up with proper procedures in order to respond with the audit recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in the DBM-DILG Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, to support the cash advance for Confidential Fund.  Henceforth, strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of disbursement vouchers for cash advance, with complete supporting documents and the liquidation thereof as provided for in the Joint Circular.
observations.managementAction: No succeeding cash advance was granted for the purpose.
observations.reason:
No Liquidation Report was submitted by management.  

Records still remain in the books as receivable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory of the agency’s property, plant and equipment and submit the inventory report. 


Require the Accounting and Property Unit to observe the procedures on the preparation, proper recording of property, plant and equipment, and the maintenance of Subsidiary  Ledgers, Property Ledger Cards  and Property Cards in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS.

Likewise, the Accounting Unit should reconcile the balances of the accounts in the general ledger against the individual subsidiary/ property ledger cards including the inventory report prepared by the Property Unit.
observations.managementAction: Management is on the course of updating its PPE records to present the same as fairly as possible on its financial statements.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to particularly work on the recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record in the appropriate PPE accounts the capital expenditures on installation/improvements totaling P2,199,566.63; and
review subsequent transactions always to determine the nature of the projects undertaken through the attached Individual Program of Work and other supporting documents to avoid erroneous recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
Review their respective records (General Ledger and Cashbook) to identify the difference of P571,626.59 by comparing the entries therein and effect the necessary adjustments.
Conduct a quarterly comparison of cashbook balances with the subsidiary accounting records every end of the month or as the need arises, and make the appropriate corrections immediately, if with sufficient basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Convene the Municipal Development Council (MDC) so as to review the necessity and viability of the projects under the Continuing Appropriations of the 20% Development Fund for inclusion in the current year AIP, otherwise, re-appropriate the unused amounts for other development projects;
Implement fully during the budget year all projects in the AIP in order to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF and achieve socio-economic, development and environment progress in the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer review the balances of the Continuing Appropriations during the preparation of the annual budget so that any amount no longer needed can be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the LGU or re-appropriated for other development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer limit the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF for purposes that are outlined in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), submit the Supplemental Investment Plan (SIP) and the related MDC and SB Resolutions which includes the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP totaling P2,537,154.91 which were not covered in SIP No. 2, 4 and 5.
We also recommended that the LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM Officer:
include only in the succeeding year’s LDRRM Investment Plan, the PPAs which are for disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness activities and stop using the LDRRMF to pay for administrative expenses in order to avoid audit disallowances; and
Include always in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to the funded from the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to:
Submit monthly to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund without need for demand;
Maintain the Subsidiary Ledgers for each type of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team at yearend.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee, through the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, comply with the posting requirements on the utilization of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities  as required in Item 4.0 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that: The Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality.  The Inventory Committee shall prepare and submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and thereafter, regularly submit such reports not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386.

The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to: (a) record the on-going projects to the CIP accounts; (b) reclassify the accounts to their appropriate asset accounts; (c) maintain subsidiary ledgers for all PPE accounts; and (d) henceforth, record financial transactions in conformity with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Combined Financial Statements the following: (a) PPE that were impaired or lost; (b) carrying amount of temporary idle PPE; (c) fully depreciated PPE that is still in use; and (d) the carrying amount of unserviceable/obsolete PPE and held for disposal.
observations.managementAction:
Management has yet to come up with actual inventory of PPE to delist non-existing assets and update the same with the new acquisition.




As to recording of on-going projects to CIP accounts, management have already complied with the recommendation.




Management has not acted upon the recommendation
observations.reason:
Lack of personnel to particularly work on the recommendation.




Lack of personnel to particularly work on the recommendation.

Updated  Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs), will be maintained after the physical inventory count.




Lack of personnel to particularly work on the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant, on a periodic basis the necessary data/reports/documents regarding market stall rentals for recording purposes. The Municipal Accountant should record in the books of accounts the outstanding receivables as Operating Lease Receivable and set up an allowance for impairment for losses which may arise from the non-collection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already submitted to the Accounting Unit the list of uncollected rentals as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not furnished the copy of JEV as proof of recording of the Income from market stall fees.

observations.managementAction: The Engineering Office has reviewed its monitoring process of ensuring that contractors will follow the time allowed for each contract in order to finish the projects on time.
observations.reason:
Although management expressed its awareness of the instances allowed by law on the grant of time extension, however no reason(s) was given for the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

No reason(s) was given by management for its non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee be required to strictly observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.  Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of perfected contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management will abide by the recommendation for the subsequent transactions.
The Municipal Accountant transferred the prior years unexpended balance in March 2020.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part II, audit finding No. 6 of this report.




Reiterated in Part II, audit finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adopt the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system and avail of the ATM facility of AGDBs to safeguard government funds and property.
observations.managementAction: Some of the employees already applied for their payroll account at the Land Bank Branch.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board appropriate funds to support the ECCD program intended for the health, nutrition, early education and social services development for young children aged zero to four years old in accordance with RA No. 10410 and DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 s. 2017.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for ECCD programs were included in the agency’s GF annual budget because management noticed that a meager resources of the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Officer in-charge of payroll preparation be advised to strictly observe the limitations on payroll deductions so that the monthly net take home pay of employees will not be reduced to an amount lower than ₽5,000.00 pursuant to Section 52 of the General Provisions of RA 11260.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to record the undistributed construction materials costing P216,457.50.  Henceforth, record purchases of construction materials as Asset first and record the corresponding Expense account only when these assets are issued to end-users, transfer or write-down.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a MOA with the Head of the School to include a provision for the latter to submit a Fund Utilization Report to ensure that funds released was utilized in accordance with the purpose.  We also recommend that adjustment be made in the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012.

Strictly adhere to Section 287 of the Local Government Code by appropriating at least 20% of the IRA for development projects and Section 322  of the same Code for the proper reversion of appropriation to the unappropriated surplus;

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer notify the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity, that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted, as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013. Likewise, regularly monitor contractor’s compliance with the posting of signboards, complete with the information, as required in the aforementioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: Management furnished only the on-going and proposed projects for CY 2020 but not the notice for signboard posting.
observations.reason: Management did not submit their explanation for the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management maintain a Special Account in the General Fund for each economic enterprise in accordance with Section 313 of RA 7160. 

Moreover, management should adopt the LEE Manual as a guide in identifying, establishing and operating viable LEEs in the municipality as prescribed in the Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommend that management appropriate in its Annual Budget in the ensuing year, one percent (1%)  of the IRA for the strengthening and implementation of the programs of the LCPC, as required in Section 15 of RA 9344. Also, prepare and submit a utilization report and the required reports enumerated in Item No. 1 of Part II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for the proper and effective implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO was required to come up with PPAs specifically for LCPC.
observations.reason: The required appropriation for LCPC was not included in the succeeding  annual budget.  Management did not submit their explanation for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of the transactions involving the project implementation particularly the receipt of fund transfer, utilization of the fund which includes the costs incurred to construct and complete the PPE, and liquidation thereof for it to be completely accounted for in the books of accounts.


Moreover, the Accountant should cause the recording of the PPE account in the books and accounts of the LGU-Barangay upon turn-over and acceptance supported by the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer had submitted the Certificates of Completion and Turn-over to COA without furnishing the Accountant a copy as his basis of recording the asset in the books and eventually record in the books of account of the barangay.
observations.reason: Management did not submit their explanation for the non-implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended that:
a. The Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee to
conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the
Municipality. The Inventory Committee shall prepare and
submit the required physical inventory reports to the Auditor and
thereafter, regularly submit such reports not later than January
31 of each year in accordance with the Handbook on Property
and Supply Management System prescribed in COA Circular No.
92-386;
b. The Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries
to: (a) record the on-going projects to the CIP accounts; (b)
reclassify the accounts to their appropriate asset accounts; (c)
maintain subsidiary ledgers for all PPE accounts; and (d)
henceforth, record financial transactions in conformity with COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015;
c. The Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Combined
Financial Statements the following: (a) PPE that were impaired
or lost; (b) carrying amount of temporary idle PPE; (c) fully
depreciated PPE that is still in use; and (d) the carrying amount
of unserviceable/obsolete PPE and held for disposal.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal
Accountant, on a periodic basis, the necessary data/reports/documents
regarding market stall rentals for recording purposes. The Municipal
Accountant should record in the books of accounts the outstanding receivables
as Operating Lease Receivable and set up an allowance for impairment for
losses which may arise from its non-collection.

3.10. We recommended that management strictly adhere to Section 325(h) of
RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, in the
appropriation and obligation of extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses
(discretionary fund) in order to prevent the incurrence of obligations without
legal basis.

4.8 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer ascertain the actual weather condition
existing in the project site through the Project Engineer In-
charge, where the projects are implemented and consider other
documentary evidence prior to approval of the contractor’s
request for time extension of work;
b. Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations on the
grant of time extension of work in accordance with Items 10.2 and
10.5 of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations (RIRR) of RA No. 9184;
c. Management should observe the procedure in determining the
contract time or duration during the conduct of detailed
engineering and disclose in the Instruction to Bidders the
estimated number of rainy/unworkable days considered
unfavorable for the prosecution of the works at the site before the
date of bidding pursuant to Item 3(j) of Annex A of the RIRR of
RA No. 9184.

4.9 This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of technical review
of contract by the COA Technical Services Office, this region.

5.9 We recommended that the Department Heads concerned be required
to submit an Accomplishment/Post-Activity Report to the COA Audit Team to
be consolidated by the GAD Focal Person in order to ascertain the propriety of
the expenditures totaling P 1,986,872.44 in accordance with Sections 4(6) and
122 of PD 1445.

6.13 We recommended that Bids and Awards Committee be required to
strictly observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive
bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A.
9184. Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of
perfected contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12,
2009.

7.12 We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund books and
revert back to the General Fund the unexpended balance of
LDRRMF for CY 2013;
b. LDRRM Council to include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
under a separate caption, the list of PPAs charged to previous years
unexpended LDRRMF balance; and
c. MDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit a monthly
Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF within the
prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8.8 We recommended that the LDRRM Council be directed to include in
its LDRRMF Investment Plan, only programs/projects/activities that are
related to disaster management activities in order to ensure that the LDRRMF
is appropriated for valid programs/projects/ activities only, pursuant to Section
21 of RA 10121, Items A & B of DILG MC No. 2012-73 and Item 5 of the
NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC No. 2013-01. Likewise, stop the practice of
charging unrelated expenses to the LDRRMF.

9.11 We recommended that management adopt the Automated Teller
Machine (ATM) payroll system and avail of the ATM facility of AGDBs to
safeguard government funds and property.

10.10 We recommended that the Local School Board appropriate funds to
support the ECCD program intended for the health, nutrition, early education
and social services development for young children aged zero to four years old
in accordance with RA No. 10410 and DepEd-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 s. 2017.

11.5 We recommended that the Officer in-charge of payroll preparation be
advised to strictly observe the limitations on payroll deductions so that the
monthly net take home pay of employees will not be reduced to an amount lower
that P5,000.00 pursuant to Section 52 of the General Provisions of RA 11260.

2. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record all purchases of drugs and medicines as well as accountable forms and postage stamps following the Report on Physical Count of Inventories submitted.
3. Likewise, require him to strictly observe the proper use of the accounts in recording financial transactions as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: No JEV was drawn for the adjustment. No report of inventory issued nor Physical Count of Inventories was submitted.
observations.reason: Purchases in CY 2018 were still recorded as outright expense.

observations.recommendation:
8. We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to submit copies of approved Purchase Orders to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from issuance pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
9. Likewise, require the official in charge of accepting deliveries of procured items to furnish the auditor copies of delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented. Concerned staff in-charge who submits the contract but not contract documents submitted the Purchase Order and Inspection and Acceptance Report. The in-charge of accepting deliveries of procured items did not notify COA within twenty-four (24) hours.

observations.recommendation: 12. We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in the DBM-DILG-GCC-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015, to support the cash advance for Confidential Fund. Henceforth, strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of disbursement vouchers for cash advance, with complete supporting documents and the liquidation thereof as provided for in the Joint Circular.
observations.managementAction: No Liquidation Report was submitted by management.
observations.reason: No succeeding cash advance was granted for this purpose.

observations.recommendation:
16. We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the 29 disbursement vouchers and 2 payrolls to support the disbursements totaling P1,365,713.29.
17. Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management has yet to submit the lacking DVs and promised to submit in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
Local Roads Asset Management System outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-
008 dated November 23, 2015 be strictly adhered to, by requiring the following
officials to:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of the local road
under its jurisdiction and submit a report thereon;
b. The Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for the
computation of depreciation and disclose the road network system
in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
c. The General Services Officer to maintain Local Road Inventory
and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card, as well as prepare a Report on Local Road Network.

2.9 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw
a journal entry voucher to record the undistributed construction materials
costing P216,457.50. Henceforth, record purchases of construction materials as
Asset first and record the corresponding Expense account only when these assets
are issued to end-users, transfer or write-down.

3.5 We recommended that management file a request for authority to write-
off dormant accounts totaling P916,731.73 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to
Section 8 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

4.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive execute a MOA with the
Head of the School to include a provision for the latter to submit a Fund
Utilization Report to ensure that funds released was utilized in accordance
with the purpose. We also recommend that adjustment be made in the
books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2012-01 dated June
14, 2012.

5.16 We recommended that management:
a. Strictly adhere to Section 287 of the Local Government Code by
appropriating at least 20% of the IRA for development projects
and Section 322 of the same Code for the proper reversion of
appropriation to the unappropriated surplus;
b. Henceforth, ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help
achieve the desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes of the LGU in accordance with Item 5.0
of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and limit the appropriation and
utilization of the 20% DF for purposes that are outlined in the
DILG-DB M JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

6.7 We recommended that the LDRRM Council be directed to include in its
LDRRMFIP, only programs/projects/activities that are related to disaster
management activities in order to ensure that the LDRRM Fund is appropriated
for valid programs/projects/ activities only, pursuant to Section 21 of RA 10121,
Items A & B of DILG MC No. 2012-73 and Item 5 of the NDRRMC/DBM/DILG
JMC No. 2013-01. Likewise, stop the practice of charging unrelated expenses to
the LDRRM Fund.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the
unexpended balance of the LDRRM Fund of CY 2014 and 2018 to the Special
Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books, and require the LDRRM Council to
present the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRM
Fund of previous years under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP. Likewise,
require the Municipal DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit a
monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund within the
prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Engineer notify the Audit Team
within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the
start of the program/activity, that the appropriate project signboards and/or
public notices are already posted, as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013. Likewise, regularly monitor contractor’s compliance
with the posting of signboards, complete with the information, as required in
the aforementioned Circular.

9.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer prepare the disbursement voucher, issue and release the related
checks for the RPT shares in order for the concerned barangays utilize their
share/income for priority projects most beneficial to its constituents.

10.6 We recommended that management maintain a Special Account in the
General Fund for each economic enterprise in accordance with Section 313 of
RA 7160.

10.7 Likewise, prepare a separate Annual Operating Budget for each
economic enterprise to be included in the General Fund’s Annual Budget.
Henceforth, salaries, wages and other related expenses of the economic
enterprise shall be charged to their respective budgets pursuant to Section
325(a) of the same Act.

10.8 Moreover, management should adopt the LEE Manual as a guide in
identifying, establishing and operating viable LEEs in the municipality as
prescribed in the Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016.
1. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an allowance for depreciation on all of the Municipality’s depreciable assets in accordance with PPSAS 17.

4. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal entry voucher to record the fund transfer to its appropriate account.
5. Henceforth, require him to strictly observe the proper use of the accounts in recording financial transactions as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009. Moreover, furnish COA with a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement as well as plans, programs and budget for the festival.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation:
6. We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat be required to strictly observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive bidding as outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 and submit documents.
7. Likewise, require them to strictly observe the timely submission of perfected contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the perfected contracts when requested.
observations.reason: Perfected contracts were not submitted to the Audit Team within five (5) days from its execution, thus precluding timely review thereof.

observations.recommendation: 10. We recommend that the management ensure optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P20,040,000.00 was in implemented for Construction project. Project is on-going as of December 31, 2018.
observations.reason: Awaiting for additional funds implemented in CY 2019 to implement the Construction project.

observations.recommendation: 11. We recommend that the management appropriate in its Annual Budget the amount of P377,000.00 for one Child Protection Program and prepare and submit a utilization report and the required reports enumerated in Item No. 1 of Part II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for the proper and effective implementation of PPAs.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the amount of P377,000.00 was allocated for one Child Protection Program.
observations.reason: Management promised to include its allocation in CY 2019 budget because the CY 2018 budget was already approved.

observations.recommendation: 13. We recommend that the Special Disbursing Officers be required to renew their fidelity bonds taking into consideration their level of cash accountability pursuant to Section 101(2) of PD 1445, Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2006-005 dated July 13, 2006, and Section 7.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

14. We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit at the beginning of the year, a list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and
15. inform COA within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the list of on-going and to be implemented projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year. The Engineer informed COA within ten (10) days after award for subsequent projects.

observations.recommendation: 18. We recommend that the management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 88 of RA 10924.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit had submitted their Unit of Implementation. Other concerned Unit of the LGU did not submit their action Plan and Status of Implementation.

observations.recommendation:
19. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer: review their respective records (GL and Cashbook) to account for the difference of P87,188.43 by comparing each other’s entries in their respective records; and
20. effect the adjustments in their respective records to come up with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance, henceforth, conduct thorough periodic reconciliation between their respective records since various errors in recording and reconciling items still subsist.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant explained that some reconciling items were from previous years that records may not be retrieved. The Accountant started the reconciliation of their books of accounts. The Municipal Treasurer had compared each to account for the difference and promised to comply by 2019.

observations.recommendation:
21. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of the transactions involving the project implementation which includes the receipt of fund transfer, utilization of the fund which includes the costs incurred to construct and complete the PPE, and record in the books of account the asset in the books of account of the barangay and eventually cause the recording of the PPE account in the books and accounts of the LGU-Barangay upon turn-over and acceptance supported by the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance to establish accountability.
22. Moreover, the Accountant should cause the recording of the PPE account in the books of the LGU upon turn-over and acceptance supported by the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit and the General Services Office furnished the Cost incurred to establish the PPE and the Project Engineer submitted the Certificate of Turn-over to COA without furnishing the Accountant a copy as his basis of recording the asset in the books. The Accountant explained that they will cause the recording upon receipt of the Certificate of Turn-over.

observations.recommendation:
23. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant use the proper accounts classification to record transactions accurately.
24. The GSO and Municipal Agriculturist submit the lacking documentation for the above transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant explained that the GSO and Municipal Agriculturist did not submit the required Report on Physical Count of Inventories. The GSO and Municipal Agriculturist promised to submit the report by 2019.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant use the proper accounts classification to record transactions accurately.
26. The Municipal Engineer had submitted the List of Concessioners/Recipients but not the required Report on Physical Count of Inventories particularly the water meters.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not submit the journal entry voucher for the recording of the transactions affected. The Municipal Engineer had submitted the List of Concessioners/Recipients but did not submit the required Report on Physical Count of Inventories.

observations.recommendation:
27. The General Services Office (GSO), being responsible in the determination of the propriety of issuance of gasoline, oil and other lubricants must maintain the needed stock cards to enable management to monitor as well as regulate their usage as prescribed in the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
28. BAC shall follow its procurement procedure in accordance with IRR and based on the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP).
observations.managementAction: Compliance with the recommended actions is likewise proposed. The GSO committed to maintain stock cards and BAC to follow procurement procedures.

observations.recommendation: 29. We recommend that the Accountant stop the practice of releasing payment without complete documentation in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012 to establish validity, regularity and propriety of these transactions.

30. We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Officer (MEO) conduct immediately an inventory on the balance of work still needed to be done to be used as basis for the preparation of Plan and POW for the provision of additional funding. The MEO should prioritize the implementation of barangay facilities exposed to elements to avoid their fast deterioration.
observations.managementAction: The MEO conducted inventory and prioritized works; implementation continued.

observations.recommendation: 31. We recommend that the LDRRMO prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 11, 2012 and certify correct the same by the Municipal Accountant and submit it to concerned offices and the COA on or before the 15th day after end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO prepared and submitted the required reports thereafter and the Municipal Accountant certified them.

observations.recommendation:
32. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory of agency’s property, plant and equipment and submit the inventory report.
33. Require the Accounting and Property Unit to observe the procedures on the preparation, proper recording of property, plant and equipment, and the maintenance of Subsidiary Ledgers, Property Ledger Cards and PARs in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS.
34. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should reconcile the balances of the accounts in the general ledger against the individual subsidiary/property ledger cards including the inventory report prepared by the Property Unit.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee had updated the PPE Ledger Cards and PARs and submitted inventory report. The Accounting Unit had submitted reconciliation and undertook adjustments. Some actions still outstanding.
observations.reason: Other concerned departments had not yet updated their records. Concerned staff committed to comply.

observations.recommendation:
35. Require the concerned municipal department officials to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects or programs in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations; and
36. Prepare liquidation report of those projects completed which were funded out of the fund under the account Due to other NGAs and Due to LGUs and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared liquidation reports for completed projects and returned balances where appropriate.

observations.recommendation:
37. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to prepare the list of creditors with valid claims supported with complete documents to prove the accuracy of the accounts payable in the books.
38. We also recommend that the necessary adjustments in the books of the Municipality be made to revert payables which had been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant prepared the list of creditors and made necessary adjustments for payables with no claims filed.

observations.recommendation: 39. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 114 and 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer complied with periodic inventory thereafter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to provide an
allowance for depreciation on all of the Municipality’s depreciable assets in
accordance with PPSAS 17.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to record all
purchases of drugs and medicines as well as accountable forms and stamps
following the perpetual inventory method. Likewise, require him to strictly
observe the proper use of the accounts in recording financial transactions as
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to draw a journal
entry voucher to record the fund transfer to its appropriate account. Henceforth,
require him to strictly observe the proper use of the accounts in recording financial
transactions as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009. Moreover, furnish COA
with a copy of the Memorandum of Agreement as well as plans, programs and
budget for the festival.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the
liquidation reports are submitted.

We recommend that the BAC and BAC Secretariat be required to strictly
observe all the procurement processes in the conduct of competitive bidding as
outlined in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 and
submit the lacking procurement documents. Likewise, require them to strictly
observe the timely submission of perfected contracts pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to
submit copies of approved Purchase Orders to the Office of the Auditor within five
(5) working days from issuance pursuant to Section 3.2.1 of COA Circular No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Likewise, require the official in charge of
accepting deliveries of procured items to furnish the auditor copies of delivery
documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods
and services pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May
18, 2009.

Management committed to institute procedures to comply with the audit
recommendation.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive ensures implementation and
optimal utilization of the 20% DF to help achieve the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the LGU pursuant to DILG-DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommend that management appropriate in its Annual Budget in the
ensuing year, one percent (1%) of the IRA for the strengthening and
implementation of the programs of the LCPC, as required in Section 15 of RA 9344
Also, prepare and submit a utilization report and the required reports enumerated
in Item No. 1 of Part II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 for the
proper and effective implementation of PPAs.

We recommend for the submission of the documents called for in the DBM-
DILG Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01
dated January 8, 2015, to support the cash advance for Confidential Fund.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the timeframe for the submission of
disbursement vouchers for cash advance, with complete supporting documents
and the liquidation thereof as provided for in the Joint Circular.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the
documents supporting the cash advance are submitted. Management committed to
comply with the audit recommendations and follow the prescribe guidelines on the
entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential funds.

We recommend that the Special Disbursing Officers be required to renew
their fidelity bonds taking into consideration their level of cash accountability
pursuant to Section 101(2) of PD 1445, Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 as
amended by COA Circular No. 2006-005 dated July 13, 2006, and Section 7.2 of
Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

Management informed that the two (2) SDOs already renewed their fidelity bond
and promised to adhere to the audit recommendation in the ensuing year.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer be required to submit at the
beginning of the year a list of on-going and to be implemented
projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for the current year, and inform COA within
ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the
program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are
already posted as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant be required to
submit the 29 disbursement vouchers and 2 payrolls to support the recorded
disbursements totaling P1,365,713.29. Likewise, require them to strictly
observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of
RA 7160, Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-
006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445. This recommendation
is without prejudice to the results of post-audit once the disbursement vouchers and
payrolls are submitted.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the
audit recommendations and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI
form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on
Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 88
of RA 10924.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 2009-02.
observations.reason: Management still does not submit copies of Purchase Orders (PO) and Inspection Acceptance Reports (IAR).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee submit supporting documents for all stages of the public bidding process as prescribed in the COA Circular No. 2009-0001 dated February 12, 2009 and RA 9184 and its IRR as amended on September 2, 2009.
observations.reason: Management did not give a valid reason on the non-implementati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant-to use the proper accounts classification to record transactions accurately.
observations.reason: During the year, purchased medicines amounting to P 1,941,502.00 were recorded as an outright expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal department officials to immediately utilize the fund for the intended projects or programs in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations; and prepare liquidation report of those projects completed which were funded out of the fund under the account Due to other NGAs and Due to LGUs and return to the source agency the balances not utilized.
observations.reason: The agency did not give a valid reason why they were not implementati on.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 114 and 124, Chapter 7, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to conduct periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials during the year and prepare the reports thereon as required.
observations.reason: The agency has not submitted a report on the physical count of inventories existing as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or the General Services Officer to initiate steps to enforce titling of real properties owned by the municipality and require him to prepare a master list of all lots owned by the Municipality indicating therein the use of said properties and whether these are revenue generating or not.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor explained that they have only certificates for tax declarations and that he finds it difficult to secure title of parcels. He promised to submit the masterlist of all lots owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
a.) review their respective records (GL and Cashbook) to account for the
difference of P87,188.43 by comparing each other’s entries in their
respective records;
b.) effect the recommended adjustments in their respective records to come up
with the accurate Cash-Local Treasury account balance;
c.) henceforth, conduct thorough periodic reconciliation between their
respective records since various errors in recording and reconciling items
still subsist.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure proper recording of the
transactions involving the project implementation particularly the receipt of fund
transfer, utilization of the fund which includes the costs incurred to construct and
complete the PPE, and liquidation thereof for it to be completely accounted for in the
books of accounts.

Moreover, the Accountant should cause the recording of the PPE account in
the books and accounts of the LGU-Barangay upon turn-over and acceptance
supported by the Certificate of Turn-over and Acceptance to establish accountability.

Hereunder are our suggested accounting entries:
Trust Fund Books:
Original Entry: Correct Entry: Adjusting Journal Entry:
1. Upon Receipt:
Cash xxx Cash xxx
Due to NGAs xxx Due to NGAs xxx
2 . Upon Construction
Due to NGAs xxx CIP – Bldg. & Other xxx CIP – Bldg. & Other xxx
Structures Structures
Cash xxx Cash / Accounts Payable xxx Due to NGAs xxx
3. Upon Completion
Buildings xxx Buildings xxx
CIP – Bldg. & Other xxx CIP – Bldg. & Other xxx
Structures Structures
4. Upon Liquidation
Due to NGAs xxx Due to NGAs xxx
Government Equity xxx Government Equity xxx
Original Entry: Correct Entry: Adjusting Journal Entry:
5. Upon Transfer to General Fund
Government Equity xxx Government Equity xxx
Buildings xxx Buildings xxx
General Fund Books:
Buildings xxx
Government
Equity xxx
Upon Transfer to the
6. Barangay
Barangay Books
Buildings xxx
Grants &
Donations in
Kind xxx

We recommend that the:
a.) Municipal Accountant use the proper accounts classification to record
transactions accurately.
