b.) GSO and Municipal Agriculturist submit the lacking documentation for
the above transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant use the proper accounts
classification to record transactions accurately.

We recommend that the Accountant prepare a separate payroll for RATA
duly supported with complete documentation in consonance with Local Budget
Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012.

We recommend that the Rural Health Unit prepare a separate payroll for
Subsistence and Laundry Allowance for review and processing of the Accounting
Unit duly supported with the required documents in consonance with DBM-DOH
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2012 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that the General Services Office submit the APP-CSE to DBM
in consonance with item 3.0 of DBM Circular Letter No. 200-6A, and henceforth,
procure all common-use supplies from the PS-DBM, if available.

We recommend that management prepare and submit all lacking
documentary requirements complete with the required data. Compliance with
hereunder courses of actions is likewise proposed, viz:
a.) The General Services Office (GSO), being responsible in the determination
of the propriety of issuance of gasoline, oil and other lubricants must
maintain the needed stock cards to enable management to monitor as well
as regulate their usage. Follow strictly the guidelines set forth in COA
Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 prescribing the use of the
“Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.”
b.) BAC shall follow its procurement procedure in accordance with IRR of RA
9184 and based on the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

We recommend that Accountant stop the practice of releasing payment
without complete documentation in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2012-001 dated June 24, 2012 to establish validity, regularity and propriety of
these transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Officer (MEO) conduct
immediately an inventory on the balance of work still needed to be done to be used as
basis for the preparation of Plan and POW for the provision of additional funding.
The MEO should prioritize the implementation of barangay facilities exposed to
elements to avoid their fast deterioration.

We recommend that the LDRRMO prepare and submit the Report on Sources
and Utilization of DRRMF as required in Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002
dated September 11, 2012 and certify correct the Municipal Accountant and submit
it to concerned offices and the COA on or before the 15th day after end of each month.

We recommend that management:
a) Submit the Annual GPB to DILG for its review and approval in
accordance with item C.6 of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No.
2013-001.
b) Submit a copy of the Annual GPB to the Audit Team within five (5)
working days from receipt of the reviewed and approved plan.
c) Likewise, submit a copy of the GAD AR within five (5) working days from
the end of January of the preceding year which shall be supported by the
following:
(1) brief summary of the reported program or project;
(2) copies of reported policy issuances;
(3) results of HDG tests; and
(4) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and
recommendations.

We recommend that management: a.) require all accountable officers to prepare accurately the monthly RAAF supported with requisition issue slips duly approved and properly filled up and the classification/type of accountable forms issued with corresponding serial numbers indicated. Also, require all receiver of accountable forms to spell-out their full name in the logbook before affixing their signatures above their printed name to pin-point accountability for accountable forms issued to accountable officers.

We recommend that management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical inventory of the agency’s property, plant and equipment and submit the inventory report.
b. Require the Accounting and Property Unit to observe the procedures on the preparation, proper recording of property, plant and equipment, and the maintenance of Subsidiary/Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS. Likewise, the Accounting Unit should reconcile the balances of the accounts in the general ledger against the individual subsidiary/property ledger cards including the inventory report prepared by the Property Unit.
observations.reason: Management had submitted AAR to June 30, 2016 to support the Briefer Report but failed to submit the inventory report at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply strictly with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of the COA/DBM/DILG/GOCC/DND dated January 8, 2015 to facilitate the immediate audit of the intelligence funds for prompt communication of audit results. Specifically, we recommend that henceforth, all expenses to be incurred for Intelligence and Confidential funds should be made through a cash advance pursuant to Section 6.1.2 of the aforementioned Joint Circular. On the other hand, all liquidations pertaining to the cash advances of this nature should be submitted directly to the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Unit, Chairman’s Office, COA, Quezon City, pursuant to Section 6.2.2.1 of the aforesaid Joint Circular.

We recommend that management: a.) Strictly adhere with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004. b.) Require the Municipal Engineer to inform the Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/project/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices had already been posted at the project site.
We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to use the proper accounts classification to record transactions accurately.

We recommend that the Human Resource Management Office of the municipal government ensure that workers under job order contracts signify their acceptance and conformity to its terms and conditions by way of affixing their signature on the space provided. Furnish the designated team of the COA copies of the JO contracts for audit as to the legality and propriety of services sought to be hired.

We recommend that management: a.) Strictly adhere with the relevant provisions of DPWH DO No. 22, series of 2015 in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract. b.) Require the concerned contractors to rectify deficiencies and/or refund excessive payment and take appropriate actions against officials and employees responsible in allowing the preparation of excessive estimates.

We recommend that management: including the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO strictly comply with necessary documentary requirements and prepare monitoring and evaluation report per DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 24 series of 2014.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to intensify efforts for the liquidation of outstanding cash advances, including the suspension of salaries of erring accountable officers, otherwise, the sanctions provided under COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 particularly Item 9.0 maybe imposed.
